pytorch.reason: Load-bearing: computes dI_c(AB|C)/dθ via central finite difference using torch tensor operations. The gradient sign determines whether relay strength θ increases or decreases the AB|C cut's I_c for Weyl-bipartite input states. This confirms the relay is actively opening the AB|C cut, not just preserving it. torch.kron, torch.linalg.eigvalsh, and tensor construction are used throughout.

pyg.reason: not required; no graph message-passing claim in this coupling sim

z3.reason: Load-bearing: encodes the structural impossibility of product-state Weyl + relay earning I_c(A|BC) > 0. Uses entropy additivity constraint: CNOT_BC preserves independence of A from BC (since CNOT does not act on A), so S(rho_ABC) = S_A + S_BC, giving I_c(A|BC) = -S_A <= 0. z3 UNSAT confirms this is a structural impossibility, not just a numerical finding. This is the primary proof form for the product-state claim.

cvc5.reason: not required; z3 encodes the product-state UNSAT claim directly

sympy.reason: Supportive: symbolically verifies the partial CNOT relay unitary U(θ). Confirms U†U=I (unitarity), U(0)=I (identity limit), U(π) lower block = -iX (Rx(π)=-iX, not X; full relay limit). Cross-validates the numpy implementation used in all numeric tests.

clifford.reason: not required; no Clifford-algebra rotor claim in this coupling lego

geomstats.reason: not required; no geodesic or Riemannian claim in this packet

e3nn.reason: not required; no equivariant network claim here

gudhi.reason: not required; no persistence diagram claim here

sympy_partial_cnot_verification.note: Rx(pi) = -iX (not X); U(pi) is an entangling gate with same structure as CNOT up to a local phase

P1_weyl_bell_earns_Ic_positive_at_2q_cut.interpretation: Weyl-chirality Bell state earns I_c > 0 at the single 2q cut. This is the baseline confirming the input state. Note: this is an entangled 2q state, not a product state. Sympy cross-checks the relay unitary used in all subsequent tests.

P2_weyl_bell_plus_relay_earns_multicut.interpretation: Weyl Bell ⊗ |0><0|_C + CNOT_BC relay: tests whether both A|BC and AB|C cuts earn positive I_c simultaneously.

P3_without_relay_AB_C_cut_not_positive.interpretation: Without relay (θ=0): rho_ABC = rho_Bell_AB ⊗ |0><0|_C. rho_C = |0><0| is pure, so S(rho_C)=0 and I_c(AB|C) = 0-0 = 0. The relay is specifically what opens the AB|C cut. A|BC and AC|B can be positive from the underlying Bell entanglement alone.

z3_unsat_structural_proof.meaning: UNSAT: product-state Weyl + relay cannot earn I_c(A|BC) > 0. CNOT_BC preserves A-independence, forcing I_c(A|BC) = -S_A <= 0.
z3_unsat_structural_proof.claim_encoded: S_ABC = S_A + S_BC (product input) AND I_c(A|BC) = S_BC - S_ABC > 0

N1_product_weyl_plus_relay_no_multicut.interpretation: Product-state Weyl + CNOT_BC relay cannot earn multi-cut I_c > 0. Entanglement in the input (not just chirality) is required. z3 UNSAT provides the structural proof.

autograd_gradient_weyl.interpretation: Negative/zero gradient: relay angle does not increase I_c(AB|C) from this initial θ.

N2_chiral_vs_nonchiral_multicut_comparis.honest_finding: Weyl (chiral) and non-chiral both have 3 active cuts. Chirality (H_L=-H_R vs H_L=H_R) provides NO additional multi-cut coverage beyond any entangled 2q state + relay. The advantage is from entanglement, not specifically from opposite Hamiltonians.
N2_chiral_vs_nonchiral_multicut_comparis.interpretation: N2 compares chiral (H_L=-H_R) vs non-chiral (H_L=H_R) entangled input + relay. A clean negative (no chirality advantage) is a valid earned result.

B1_maximally_mixed_no_positive_Ic_cuts.interpretation: Maximally mixed state: I_c < 0 at all cuts (entropy-suppressed). Mixed state has no positive I_c at any bipartition. This is the floor: no I_c > 0 without entanglement in the input.

B2_theta_to_zero_recovers_2q_limit.interpretation: At θ→0 the relay is identity; 3q state is rho_AB ⊗ |0><0|_C. I_c(A|BC) should converge to the 2q I_c(A->B). Confirms the relay → no-relay limit is continuous.
